description_long: This is a two-story museum building with a wide, gently sloped hip roof in a light brown color, extending well beyond the walls to provide ample overhang. The facade features a prominent entrance with large glass windows and doors, flanked by horizontal bands of red and white, giving the structure a modern yet institutional appearance. The upper floor is lined with a series of evenly spaced windows, allowing natural light to flood the interior exhibition spaces. The side and rear views reveal a more utilitarian design, with minimal fenestration and a focus on solid wall surfaces. The building's proportions are broad and low, emphasizing horizontal lines and stability. The materials suggest a combination of concrete, glass, and metal, typical for contemporary public buildings.
description: A modern, two-story museum with a wide hip roof, large glass entrance, and horizontal bands of red and white. The structure features extensive windows on the front and a solid, understated rear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large glass windows, and horizontal color bands.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep roof overhang and solid wall with minimal features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, windowless wall and the full span of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with the roof overhang and solid wall visible.
